channel.title: Financial-Operations on JIF Cookbook
channel.description: Recent content in Financial-Operations on JIF Cookbook

item.title: Budget and Funding Framework
item.description: Budget and Funding Framework The program operates on a per-cohort basis. Cost models vary significantly by city, cohort size, and local cost of living. The 2022 program cost structure is documented below as a reference point; salary ranges, housing costs, and stipends will change by location and year. Direct Fellowship Costs Per-Fellow Cost Breakdown (2022 Jerusalem Reference Model) For a 10-week international residency, budget the following per Fellow: Airfare (round-trip, international): $2,000 Ground transportation (airport pickup, local taxis, tours): $300 Housing (12 weeks, shared apartment, utilities included): $7,500 Artist stipend, per diem, and materials: $8,500 Insurance (international travel and residency coverage): $500

item.title: Jerusalem International Fellows: Financial Materials Inventory
item.description: Jerusalem International Fellows: Financial Materials Inventory Drive Search Results | July 19, 2026 EXECUTIVE SUMMARY Drive search completed successfully. Recovered comprehensive financial planning documents spanning 2014-2026, including strategic projections, actual budgets (2014-2018), and a detailed 2026 replication model. Critical findings: 2014-2018 American Academy in Jerusalem (AAJ) complete budget plan located (scanned from physical document) 2026 financial model framework (complete) already drafted by Alexandra 2022 actual P&L remains MISSING Donor lists exist but are incomplete (foundation + individual donor names identified) No evidence of detailed expense receipts or 2022 final accounting IN HAND: DOCUMENTS RECOVERED 1.

item.title: Jerusalem International Fellows: Financial Operations Playbook
item.description: Jerusalem International Fellows: Financial Operations Playbook Version 1.0 | July 19, 2026 Status: Phase 1 (Structural) complete. Phase 2 (Reconciliation) built with verification flags for Elise data. Prepared by: Alexandra Nyashina, ANyashina LLC Prepared for: Elise Bernhardt, Jerusalem International Fellows DOCUMENT ARCHITECTURE This playbook captures the complete financial operations model for JIF replication. It is structured in two layers: PHASE 1: Structural models and frameworks (rates, FTE calculations, cost templates) — these are stable across iterations PHASE 2: Reconciliation and projections — flagged with what data is pending from Elise’s 2022 actuals All projections are built from the 2026 Financial Model Framework (Kyiv/Krakow/Chicago templates).
